Add a WireGuard or Tailscale profile, th: Add a WireGuard or Tailscale profile, then choose a SOCKS5 host and port for that profile.
Allow LAN access while using exit node: Allow LAN access while using exit node
Closing the window hides it when tray su: Closing the window hides it when tray support is available. Use Quit from the tray menu to stop all profiles, wait briefly for them to close, and exit.
Connect starts the embedded backend and: Connect starts the embedded backend and SOCKS5 listener for the selected profile. WireGuard profiles use the embedded WireGuard engine. Tailscale profiles use an embedded tsnet node. The app does not launch the wireproxy, tailscale, or tailscaled command-line tools. Connect All starts every saved profile. Runtime status is shown in the selected profile log.
Tailscale auth is not the same as WireGu: Tailscale auth is not the same as WireGuard config. Paste an auth key and click Login or Connect to register this app as a Tailscale device; leave Auth key empty for a browser sign-in URL in the profile log. If Tailscale requires device approval, the profile log will ask you to approve the device in the admin console and the app will detect approval automatically. After authentication succeeds, the app removes the saved auth key, marks the profile authenticated, and keeps the auth field locked until you click Logout. Logout removes this profile's stored Tailscale state and unlocks auth. Auth key, control URL, hostname, backend, and SOCKS5 bind fields are locked while that profile is connected, connecting, or disconnecting. Exit-node mode, selected exit node, and LAN access can be changed while connected; click Save to apply them without reconnecting.
Exit-node choices are not live-updating. Connect the Tailscale profile, click Refresh to load available exit-node devices from that tailnet, and click Refresh again after tailnet devices or approvals change. You can also type a node ID, hostname, or Tailscale IP manually. Automatic exit asks Tailscale to choose an available exit node.
Connect first, then refresh to list devi: Connect first, then refresh to list devices from this tailnet. Save while connected to apply exit-node changes immediately.
Disconnect a connected or connecting pro: Disconnect a connected or connecting profile, and wait for it to finish disconnecting, before changing its backend, tunnel configuration, or SOCKS5 bind address. Profile name and startup preference can be changed while connected.
Each connected, connecting, or disconnec: Each connected, connecting, or disconnecting profile must use a unique SOCKS5 bind address, such as 127.0.0.1:1080 and 127.0.0.1:1081.
Automatic exit asks Tailscale to choose: Automatic exit asks Tailscale to choose an available exit node.
Import and Export open the operating sys: Import and Export open the operating system's native file dialog and use the selected filesystem path. Export writes profiles as JSON. Import accepts exported JSON bundles or WireGuard .conf files. Tailscale node state and the local authenticated marker are not exported, so imported Tailscale profiles require Login again.
Name: Name
Only needed for a custom control server.
Optional; paste auth key, or leave empty: Optional; paste auth key, or leave empty for browser sign-in
Paste an auth key, then click Login. Lea: Paste an auth key, then click Login. Leave it empty to use the browser sign-in URL in the profile log. After authentication succeeds, the saved auth key is removed and this field stays locked until Logout. Logout removes this profile's stored Tailscale state and unlocks auth.
Refresh to choose a device, or type a no: Refresh to choose a device, or type a node/IP
SOCKS5 port must be a number: SOCKS5 port must be a number
SOCKS5 port must be between 1 and 65535: SOCKS5 port must be between 1 and 65535
Tailscale has existing profile state; au: Tailscale has existing profile state; auth key was not used for this start
Tailscale authenticated; auth key remove: Tailscale authenticated; auth key removed from saved profile
Tailscale exit node must be automatic or: Tailscale exit node must be automatic or a specific node, not both
Tailscale login complete; approve this d: Tailscale login complete; approve this device in the Tailscale admin console. The app will continue automatically after approval
Tailscale login is waiting for an auth k: Tailscale login is waiting for an auth key or browser sign-in URL
The profile log follows the newest line: The profile log follows the newest line by default. Scrolling up pauses following so you can read earlier output; scrolling back to the bottom follows new lines again.
The tray menu has one profile row per pr: The tray menu has one profile row per profile. The colored dot shows connection status, and each submenu shows status text, SOCKS5 bind address, backend detail, and Connect or Disconnect actions. The icon is green only while connected; other states use a red icon and the status text shows disconnected, connecting, disconnecting, or error.
Used when registering this app as a Tail: Used when registering this app as a Tailscale device.
